items.md:
*Changes*: Barks submitted this story as eight pages; it was cut to six by his editors. The positions of panels 5 and 6 and panels 7 and 8 were reversed on the fifth page of the story as published.
*Reprinted*: Donald Duck No. 157, July 1974.
items.value:

items.md: Front cover: The nephews blow over their birthday cake, to Donald's dismay. (*Sept. 15, 1955*)
items.value: Front cover: The nephews blow over their birthday cake, to Donald's dismay. (Sept. 15, 1955)

items.md: *Changes*: The nephews' faces were redrawn by another artist after Barks had submitted the cover to Western.
items.value: Changes: The nephews' faces were redrawn by another artist after Barks had submitted the cover to Western.

items.md: *Reprinted*: Donald Duck No. 154, January 1974.
items.value: Reprinted: Donald Duck No. 154, January 1974.

items.md: DONALD DUCK in Secret of Hondorica - 24 - Scrooge sends the ducks deep into the jungles of Hondorica to recover valuable papers from a crashed mail plane, but Gladstone is a step ahead of them all the way. (*Sept. 30, 1954*)

items.md: *Changes*: Barks's list shows a full page "new hat" gag accepted on May 24, 1956, with no issue specified. The gag as published has obviously been cut from a full page.

items.md: DONALD DUCK and the Lost Peg Leg Mine - 10 - The ducks and Scrooge go to an abandoned mine in search of gold left there by an old prospector and find that pack rats have taken all of it. (*June 14, 1956*)
items.value: DONALD DUCK and the Lost Peg Leg Mine - 10 - The ducks and Scrooge go to an abandoned mine in search of gold left there by an old prospector and find that pack rats have taken all of it. (June 14, 1956)

items.md: This story was originally scheduled for *Walt Disney's Comics* No. 203, August 1957. In a letter dated June 22, 1956, Chase Craig informed Barks of the change and asked for another ten-page story for *Walt Disney's*.
items.value: This story was originally scheduled for Walt Disney's Comics No. 203, August 1957. In a letter dated June 22, 1956, Chase Craig informed Barks of the change and asked for another ten-page story for Walt Disney's.

items.md: ### Donald Duck in "Forbidden Valley"
items.value: Donald Duck in "Forbidden Valley"

items.md: DONALD DUCK in "Forbidden Valley" - 26 - The ducks are sent to South America to find rare wasps needed to save Duckburg's pickle crop from a bug called the augur-nosed pickle hater. In the Amazon jungle, they must contend with dinosaurs, fierce Indians, and an embittered canner named P. J. McBrine. (*Dec. 13, 1956*)
items.value: DONALD DUCK in "Forbidden Valley" - 26 - The ducks are sent to South America to find rare wasps needed to save Duckburg's pickle crop from a bug called the augur-nosed pickle hater. In the Amazon jungle, they must contend with dinosaurs, fierce Indians, and an embittered canner named P. J. McBrine. (Dec. 13, 1956)

items.value: DONALD DUCK The Master Glasser - 5 - art only - Donald, a master at repairing broken glass, breaks glass all over Duckburg with a huge tuning fork. (May 20, 1959)

items.md:
Barks: "The 'Master Glasser' story was from a script sent to me from the office. As usual, I did lots of rewriting to pull the gags together." (June 9, 1968, letter to Michael Barrier)
*Reprinted*: Donald Duck No. 188, October 1977.
